name: Classic Spinach Lasagna
recipeIngredient: 2 oz Chicken livers, cleaned and minced
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the meat sauce:
- Place dried mushrooms in a bowl and pour 1 cup boiling water over them. Let sit for 30 minutes, then drain, reserving the water.
- Rinse mushroom pieces thoroughly, chop fine, rinse again to remove all traces of grit.
- Strain the mushroom-soaking liquid into a small bowl.
- Heat olive oil in a large non-reactive skillet. Add pancetta and onion; sauté over low heat for 5 minutes.
- Raise heat to medium, crumble ground beef in, add 1/4 teaspoon salt and several grindings of fresh pepper. Sauté 5 more minutes, stirring to break up the meat.
- Add chopped chicken livers and porcini.
- Dissolve tomato paste with half cup mushroom-soaking liquid and hot water; add to skillet. Bring to a simmer, cover pan, and cook for 1 hour, stirring occasionally and regulating heat to maintain gentle simmer.
2. Prepare the spinach filling:
- If using fresh spinach: Wash carefully and place in a pot with clinging water. Sprinkle lightly with salt, cover, and bring to high boil. As soon as spinach wilts, reduce heat to medium and cook for 5 minutes.
- (Frozen spinach): Cook according to package directions. Drain in colander, spray with cold water, squeeze out most water, chop fine.
- Combine drained chopped spinach, ricotta, egg, salt, and nutmeg in a bowl; stir well.
3. Prepare the lasagna:
- Bring large pot of salted water to boil.
- Fill a large bowl with ice water.
- Lay several dish towels on work surface. Drop lasagna noodles into boiling water for 1 minute, then drain using slotted spoon. Dip in ice water and lay out on dish towels while preparing other ingredients.
4. Assemble and bake:
- Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
- Butter a 10-by-14-inch baking dish.
- Layer with about a quarter of lasagna noodles, half of the spinach mixture, grated Parmigiano, meat sauce, repeat layers until all ingredients are used up, ending with cheese and sauce on top.
- Dot remaining tablespoon butter over top. Bake for 15 minutes or until bubbling hot; let sit for 10 minutes before serving.
